title: Leymus klokovii
intro: Leymus klokovii, also known as the grass of the genus Leymus, is a perennial grass native to Eastern Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Leymus klokovii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leymus klokovii is commonly used in herbal medicine, with preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures being made to harness its therapeutic potential.
taxonomy: Leymus klokovii is classified within the family Poaceae. Specifically, it is a member of the tribe Triticeae. Further, it is placed in the genus Leymus, which is a group of grasses native to the Northern Hemisphere. Leymus klokovii is also known as a wild rye or a grassy perennial that is native to the steppes and meadows of Eurasia. Its classification is also recognized as a perennial grass in the flora of various regions.

parts: Leymus klokovii. The medicinal parts of Leymus klokovii, also known as the Kozlov's wildrye, are primarily its leaves and roots.
